description:
§22.4 runtime (b) — a compile-time `emit()` inlines its markup into the rendered tree, so the post-run DOM carries the emitted `

hi

`. Pins the (b) RUNTIME EFFECT of compile-time emit: the ordinary run() renders it (no new driver).
rationale:
§22.4: the compiler SHALL evaluate compile-time meta blocks during compilation and INLINE the result. A compile-time `emit(html)` splices `html` into the AST/rendered tree at compile time (per §22.4.1 it passes through the block splitter + AST builder), so the emitted `

hi

` is present in the final rendered DOM.
